modeDescription: The migration gate is a hard non-mutating production promotion gate. Unauthorized runtime ensure paths fail promotion. Temporary allowlist entries cannot certify production promotion. Runtime ensure residuals must be covered by reviewed additive migration evidence or machine-governed residual ledger evidence.

approvedRuntimeEnsureInventory.reason: Highest-risk production ingest queue runtime ensure path is now represented by a reviewed additive migration while the runtime ensure remains as compatibility redundancy.
Runtime ensure compatibility path is governed by the machine-validated residual ledger; future narrowing remains a separate focused additive migration task.

forbiddenMutationPatterns.reason: Destructive DDL requires a separate reviewed migration and rollback plan.
Any schema mutation source outside approved inventory fails the gate.
Every schema mutation source must be named in the migration ledger.
Bootstrap DDL must remain explicitly approved and non-production.
Production promotion must fail when a runtime ensure path is not covered by reviewed additive migration evidence or machine-governed residual ledger evidence.

releaseGateRequirements: Run the production promotion script gate before pushing to the production repository.
Unknown schema mutation source count must be zero.
Unledgered schema mutation source count must be zero.
Missing expected source count must be zero.
Missing expected inventory entry count must be zero.
Bootstrap mutation sources must be limited to allowedBootstrapScripts.
Unauthorized runtime ensure source count must be zero.
Converted runtime ensure entries must reference reviewed additive migration files.
Machine-governed runtime residual entries must include ownerRole, reason, ledgerEntry, ledgerStatus, productionPromotionAuthorized:true, and CERTIFYING:true.
Active temporary runtime ensure allowlist entries are release-blocking production promotion residuals and cannot certify production promotion.
Expired temporary runtime ensure allowlist entries must fail with expired status.
The gate must remain non-mutating and must not connect to a database.
CERTIFYING and productionPromotionGateAccepted must remain false while any temporary runtime ensure allowlist entry is active.
CERTIFYING may be true only when every runtime residual is ledgered additive or reviewed and governed.
waiverRequirements: Formal waiver mode is not the current production promotion gate policy.
Any future waiver must be explicit, expiring, non-mutating, and unable to authorize unknown, unledgered, missing, or unapproved schema mutation sources.
futureCutoverProcedure: Select one machine-governed runtime ensure source when ready for narrowing and create a reviewed additive migration entry.
Name affected tables, indexes, runtime ensure function, rollback notes, and verification commands.
Keep the runtime ensure function active until redundant-additive behavior is proven.
Only remove or narrow an ensure path in a separate task with focused tests for that runtime path.
Temporary allowlist entries are not a certifying production promotion path.
